records.ety: [OE. brok, broke, brook, AS. brōc; akin to D. broek, LG. brōk, marshy ground, OHG. pruoh, G. bruch marsh; prob. fr. the root of E. break, so as that it signifies water breaking through the earth, a spring or brook, as well as a marsh. See Break, v. t.]

defs: A natural stream of water smaller than a river or creek.
quotes: The Lord thy God bringeth thee into a good land, a land of brooks of water.
Empires itself, as doth an inland brook Into the main of waters.

records.ety: [OE. broken, bruken, to use, enjoy, digest, AS. brūcan; akin to D. gebruiken to use, OHG. prūhhan, G. brauchen, gebrauchen, Icel. br<?/ka, Goth. br<?/kjan, and L. frui, to enjoy. Cf. Fruit, Broker.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Brooked (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Brooking.]

defs: To bear; to endure; to put up with; to tolerate; as, young men can not brook restraint.
quotes: Shall we, who could not brook one lord, Crouch to the wicked ten?
